# Copilot Instructions

## Terraform Provider

- This is a Terraform Provider. All code must be written in Go, and follow the conventions of Terraform Providers. See
  the [Terraform Plugin SDK documentation](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/providers/sdk) for
  reference and [Best Practices](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/plugin/best-practices).

## Naming conventions

- When aliasing a Go import with multiple words, use underscores (e.g. `sentry_util`, `consumer_events`), not camelCase.
- File names: lowercase with underscores (e.g. `account_emails.go`, `payment_event_email.go`). Go package directories
  stay lowercase without underscores.

## Constant & variable naming

- Use **SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE** for constants (e.g. `DEFAULT_CORS_SETTINGS`, `MIN_DONATION_AMOUNT`, `CURRENCY`).
- Group related `var` or `const` declarations with parenthesized blocks:
  ```go
  var (
      loaded  = false
      locales = map[string]*gotext.Locale{}
  )
  ```

## Commit message style

- Prefer Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `test:`, `refactor:`.

## Godoc

- Every exported type, function, method, variable, and constant **must** have a godoc comment.
- Format: `// SymbolName does X.` â€” start with the symbol name, end with a period.
- Package-level comments go on the `package` line in one file per package.
- Generated files (`*.gen.go`) are exempt.

## Documentation

- Provider docs in `docs/` are auto-generated by `tfplugindocs`. Do not edit them manually. Regenerate locally with
  `make docs` and commit the result â€” CI fails the `check-docs` job if the committed docs drift from the generated
  output.
- After regenerating docs with `make docs`, always format them with `npx prettier --write docs/` before committing.

## Resource examples

- Every resource and data source **must** have a corresponding example Terraform snippet under `examples/`.
- Resource examples go in `examples/resources/<resource_type>/resource.tf` (e.g.
  `examples/resources/balena_application/resource.tf`).
- Data source examples go in `examples/data-sources/<data_source_type>/data-source.tf`.
- When adding a new resource or data source, always create its example file in the same PR.
- When changing a resource or data source schema (adding/removing/renaming attributes), update its example to reflect the
  current schema.

## Logging

- Always use `log/slog` for all logging. Never use `fmt.Print*`, `log.Print*`, or any other printing mechanism for log
  output.
- **Never include dynamic content in the log message string.** The first argument (the message) must always be a static
  string literal. All dynamic data must be passed as structured key-value fields.
- **Always** use `.With()` to attach dynamic data. Never pass key-value pairs inline as trailing arguments:

  ```go
  // CORRECT â€” static message, dynamic data via .With()
  slog.With("order_id", orderID).Error("failed to process order", "error", err)
  slog.With("amount", amount, "customer_id", customerID).Info("payment processed")

  // WRONG â€” dynamic content interpolated into the message
  slog.Info(fmt.Sprintf("payment of %d processed for %s", amount, customerID))
  slog.Info("payment processed for " + customerID)

  // WRONG â€” key-value pairs passed as trailing inline arguments
  slog.Info("payment processed", "amount", amount, "customer_id", customerID)
  ```

- Use context-aware variants (`slog.InfoContext`, `slog.ErrorContext`, etc.) when a `context.Context` is available, to
  propagate correlation IDs automatically.
- Call `app_log.Init()` (from `util/log`) at the top of every `main()` function before any logging occurs.
- **Exception â€” Terraform provider package (`internal/provider/`):** the Terraform Plugin Framework routes log output
  through [`tflog`](https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/hashicorp/terraform-plugin-log/tflog) so that Terraform CLI can
  surface and filter per-provider logs correctly. Inside the provider package, use `tflog.Trace`, `tflog.Debug`,
  `tflog.Info`, `tflog.Warn`, and `tflog.Error` (passing the request `context.Context`) instead of `slog`. The same
  "static message + structured fields" rules apply:

  ```go
  // CORRECT â€” inside internal/provider/
  tflog.Debug(ctx, "reading application", map[string]any{"application_id": id})

  // WRONG â€” dynamic content in the message
  tflog.Debug(ctx, fmt.Sprintf("reading application %d", id))
  ```

  The rest of the codebase (CLI entry points, `internal/balena/` HTTP client, tooling) continues to use `log/slog`.

## YAML (GitHub Actions workflows)

- Blank line between all top-level keys (`name`, `on`, `permissions`, `env`, `concurrency`, `jobs`).
- 2-space indentation throughout.
- **YAML anchors are mandatory** for any step or block that appears more than once. Define the anchor (`&name`) on the
  first occurrence and use aliases (`*name`) everywhere else.

## Comments

- **Do not use section divider comments.** No banner-style separators like `// ----`, `# ----`, or similar decorative
  lines to split code into sections. Let the code structure speak for itself.

## Shell & CLI output

- **Do not emit decorative headers or labeled output blocks.** No `=== Name ===`, `--- Name ---`, `### Name ###`, or
  similar banner-style echo/print lines in shell commands, scripts, or CLI output. Keep output minimal and functional.
